description_long: This megaphone features a cylindrical body painted in a dark green color with bold red bands at both ends and around the center. One side is decorated with a stylized shark mouth graphic, complete with sharp white teeth and an aggressive eye, reminiscent of WWII fighter plane nose art. The mouthpiece is conical and white, protruding from the front, while the rear opening is wide and ringed in red. A small olive-green handle is attached beneath the body, likely for gripping or carrying. The surface appears to be made of painted metal, with some visible scratches and wear, suggesting frequent use. No visible speaker grill pattern or branding is present, and the overall design is robust and utilitarian.
description: A cylindrical megaphone with red and green paint, shark mouth art, and a conical white mouthpiece. It has a sturdy handle and a bold, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the shark mouth graphic, red bands, and the olive-green handle beneath the body.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed white mouthpiece and the concentric red rings around the opening.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with red bands and green body, no shark mouth graphic visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the wide, hollow red-rimmed opening and the interior of the megaphone.
attribute_mouthpiece_design: Conical, white, protruding from the front, smooth and pointed with no visible attachments.
attribute_branding_or_logo: Shark mouth graphic with eye; no text or logo visible.
